nativeRange.habitat: A cultivated variety with no true wild population, derived from the South American Helanthium bolivianum complex that grows naturally in tropical marshes, riverbanks, and seasonally flooded wetlands.

summary: The Vesuvius Sword is a cultivated variety of the South American sword plant group, recognized by its narrow, spiraling bright-green leaves. Generally considered easy to keep, it works well as a midground accent in medium to large planted tanks.
careNotes:
TAXONOMY NOTE: 'Vesuvius' is a cultivated variety of the South American sword complex traditionally sold as Echinodorus angustifolius, now generally placed under Helanthium bolivianum. Its narrow blades grow in a distinctive tight corkscrew or spiral, setting it apart visually from the broader-leaved wild forms of its parent species — it is consistently sold and kept as its own variety rather than as a standard chain sword.

Medium light is the consensus recommendation across care guides, and the plant generally tolerates a somewhat wider range from low-to-medium up to moderate. CO2 supplementation is not required but has been reported to improve both growth rate and leaf density. As with all sword plants in this group, the Vesuvius Sword is a heavy ROOT FEEDER — a nutrient-rich substrate and regular root tabs are commonly recommended for best results.

The spiral leaf form is most visually striking when the plant has open space around it, so midground placement in medium to large tanks or background placement in smaller nano setups tends to work best. Newly purchased plants are typically grown out of water at the farm and arrive with broader, flatter leaves, sometimes with flower buds; these leaves will melt back as narrower, twisted submerged leaves emerge. This is expected behaviour, not a sign of poor health.
breedingNotes: Propagates readily by sending out runners (stolons) that produce daughter rosettes along the substrate. Once each plantlet has its own small rosette and visible roots it can be separated from the runner and replanted. Mature clumps can also be divided at the crown. Flowering and seed production occur mainly in emersed culture and are uncommon in submerged aquarium setups.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Trade-source product page for Helanthium 'Vesuvius' (formerly Echinodorus Vesuvius). Confirmed bright-green curling leaf habit, low-to-medium lighting, CO2 not necessary, easy care, heavy root feeder needing nutrient-dense substrate, propagation by runner-split, pH 5–7. Used in place of the assigned Tropica primary because Tropica has no dedicated plant-details page for this cultivar (only an incidental layout mention) — the search at tropica.com returned no usable Vesuvius product page.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op — Echinodorus Vesuvius
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing (25–30 cm / 10–12 in), narrow spiral leaf morphology, medium light preference (tolerates low to high), CO2 optional, heavy root feeder, propagation by runners similar to vallisneria, midground for medium-large tanks / background for nano tanks, easy care level, emersed-to-submersed melt-back note.

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net — Echinodorus angustifolius
additional.notes: Used for the parent species water parameters and family: Alismataceae, temperature 21–30 °C, pH 5.7–7.4, hardness 4–18 °dGH, medium light, runners propagation, easy difficulty. Size reported 5–30 cm for the wild form.

additional.name: Aqualand Pets+ — Helanthium Vesuvius
additional.notes: Secondary care guide. Reported size 10–20 cm, temp 22–28 °C, pH 6.0–7.5, hardness 3–10 dGH, moderate-to-high lighting (8–10 h), moderate CO2 benefit, runners + crown division propagation. Rates difficulty 'moderate' — outlier vs the easy ratings of Buce Plant and Aquarium Co-Op; corpus average kept at beginner.

additional.name: Aquarium Lesson — Helanthium bolivianum Angustifolius
additional.notes: Taxonomic anchor: confirms Helanthium bolivianum 'Angustifolius' as the parent species (family Alismataceae, order Alismatales) and treats 'Vesuvius' as a related cultivar of the same species. Parameters for the parent species: temp 20–26 °C, pH 5–7.5, medium light, CO2 10–40 mg/L recommended, rosette growth, midground/background placement, easy.
